data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-13T11:31:12.804Z</current_datetime>

I have this MSTest data-driven test using DynamicData with object arrays. Is there a more type-safe way to write this in modern MSTest?

```csharp
[TestMethod]
[DynamicData(nameof(TestData))]
public void Validate_ReturnsExpected(string input, bool expected)
{
    var result = Validator.IsValid(input);
    Assert.AreEqual(expected, result);
}

public static IEnumerable<object[]> TestData =>
[
    ["valid@email.com", true],
    ["invalid", false],
    ["", false],
];
```

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a more type-safe way to write DynamicData tests in modern MSTest, specifically moving away from `IEnumerable<object[]>`. This is about MSTest v3+ which introduced `ValueTuple` support for `DynamicData`.

In MSTest v3 (and further improved in v4), you can use `IEnumerable<(T1, T2, ...)>` (ValueTuples) instead of `IEnumerable<object[]>`. This gives you compile-time type safety.

Let me provide the answer directly since this is a knowledge question about MSTest features.
